thesis: PHI reused a prior optimization descriptor across compilation variants without full rediscovery.

target_binary.note: Maximally-optimized fair baseline — same compiler, full auto-detection

ggml_vec_dot_q8_0_q8_0.difference: bit 0 of Rd field — register 3 → register 2 (Rd-rename only)

phase1_result.method: exact byte comparison at mutation site
phase1_result.finding: Codegen changed across build variants; exact transfer is brittle

verdicts.delta: bit 0 of Rd field: register 3 → register 2

native_on_wall_clock.correctness_analysis: Disassembly shows instr[30] is a necessary accumulator zero-init in this build variant, not a dead write. LTO restructured the loop: ldp q4,q2 loads INT8 data into v2; sdot v4.4s,v2.16b uses v2 as data input; mov v2.16b,v6.16b (instr[30]) then zeros v2 for sdot v2.4s,v16.16b (accumulator). Removing the zero-init causes sdot to accumulate into raw INT8 bytes instead of zero — wrong result. The 1.119× throughput is from computing incorrect output faster. Oracle on target would fail.
native_on_wall_clock.conclusion: Phase 2 gate correctly passed (opcode-class compatible, Rd-rename only). Oracle on target binary is required before deployment and would reject this transfer. Phase 2 is a pre-filter, not a deployment gate.

verdicts.delta: bit 3 of Rd field in instr[1]

wall_clock_result.interpretation: Flat within noise. NATIVE=ON compiler already scheduled optimally.

conclusions.phase2_role: Pre-filter only. Passing Phase 2 means the mutation is architecturally compatible (opcode class, register rename). It does not mean the instruction is dead in the target context.
conclusions.dead_at_30_on_native_on: NOT dead. LTO restructured the loop such that instr[30] serves as sdot accumulator zero-init. Oracle on NATIVE=ON target would fail. Wall-clock 1.119x is from computing incorrect output.
conclusions.optimization_transfer_claim: PHI correctly stored and retrieved the mutation descriptor. Phase 2 gate correctly identified architectural compatibility. Oracle on target is the deployment gate — it would reject dead@30 on NATIVE=ON/LTO=ON because the instruction is not dead in that build variant.
conclusions.next_step: Phase 3 — behavior-based recognition without symbol name. Separately: find a mutation that passes Phase 2 AND oracle on NATIVE=ON target.
